title: Case 1 — everything fires
demonstrates: All four outputs land. The NGS supports the diagnosis, sets the risk tier, opens a targeted therapy, and hands over an MRD marker. Two teaching points hide in it: the quoted FLT3-ITD allelic ratio no longer changes the tier under ELN 2022, and DNMT3A at 46% VAF looks like a perfect MRD marker and is not one.
clinical_problem: Six weeks of worsening fatigue, bruising on the shins, gums bleeding when brushing. No fever. No prior haematological history, no prior chemotherapy or radiotherapy.
film: Leucoerythroblastic. 45% circulating blasts, medium-sized, high N:C ratio, prominent nucleoli, occasional Auer rods. Cup-shaped nuclei in a subset. Reduced platelets, no schistocytes.
marrow_diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukaemia. Hypercellular marrow, 62% myeloblasts. Karyotype: 46,XX [20] — normal.
